title: A Nice Place to Live
description: Nostalgia is cheap, and plenty of films peddling it have died on the vine. What sets Back to the Future apart is not that it is nostalgic, but that it grasps nostalgia as a production, a site of labor. It doesn’t remember the past; it re-manufactures it, to spec, on a studio lot.
hook: Back to the Future doesn't remember the past—it manufactures it on a studio lot, turning nostalgia into visible labor.

title: Restaurant Review: Sanwits
description: Sanwits, in the old Rowdy Rooster flagship spot, serves a glorious mashup of American sandwich styles and frank Filipino flavors.
hook: A sandwich shop that marries American styles with frank Filipino flavors—culinary hybridism that tastes like genuine cultural collision.

title: The Importance of Ma: Spirited Away at 25
description:
Rory Kiberd extols the virtues of space contained within Hayao Miyazaki's teeming but brilliant anime about childhood & maturation

Including a spoiler warning for Spirited Away would be farcical. Hayao Miyazaki’s animated film is impossible to spoil, let alone explain its narrative beats retroactively. Like a dream,
hook: Spirited Away at 25: how Miyazaki's animation uses negative space to capture the interior experience of becoming a woman.
